summary: Independent QA accepts the M7 visual/UX handoff and the systems ACCEPT envelope as coordination-ready. The handoff can be converted into downstream M7 UI/localization quality-track tasks only if those tasks inherit the architecture, i18n, read-model, Human Gate, and non-implementation conditions recorded here and in the systems review.

tests_run.result: Only unrelated untracked M7-BETA-CULTURE-REVIEW-001 QA handoff JSON was present before this QA handoff was written.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for task, visual handoff, and systems handoff

tests_run.result: M7-VISUAL-UX-HANDOFF-001 and M7-BETA-CULTURE-REVIEW-001 are REVIEW; prior M7 content-lock contract, scenario fill, tutorial, and audio/art/localization prerequisites are CLOSED.
M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001 is the only current ready task; the visual/UX handoff remains review-gated until lead integration.
Confirmed cited map selection and M3 appointment command surfaces exist in client-core/ui/map-renderer/tests.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse check for QA handoff

tests_run.result: Final status after writing this QA handoff also showed parallel project state/culture-review modifications and untracked culture-review handoff files outside this visual/UX QA artifact.
Final whitespace check still reported no errors.

decisions.decision: Accept the visual handoff and systems review as adequate to drive downstream M7 UI/localization planning. This is not implementation approval, content-lock acceptance, production art approval, release approval, or Human Gate approval.

The visual handoff maps concept-art intent to existing or already-designed read models, UI surfaces, command factories, debug surfaces, and asset slots. Target-expression items must remain UI/layout/copy/token work unless a later task explicitly approves new domain capability.

Downstream UI/localization tasks must keep i18n outside sim-core, keep React/client state outside authoritative WorldState ownership, treat Pixi as rebuildable render cache, preserve SimulationPort/GameCommand/query boundaries, and keep player and AI on the same command surface.

The task status REVIEW and allowed paths are appropriate for a coordination-only handoff. It performs no UI, i18n, token, asset, app-shell, package, sim rule, content-source, release, telemetry, server, multiplayer, cloud-save, account, secret, or manual node battle implementation.

Formal content lock, major cultural-risk symbols/assets, manual playthrough acceptance requiring human judgment, manual node battle reversal, cloud-save/accounts/telemetry/paid services/secrets, commercial/release/branding decisions, arbitrary-code mods, server/multiplayer, irreversible migration, and frozen architecture or historical-policy changes remain Human Gates.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE. The handoff correctly classifies manual node battle UI as rejected for the 1.0 mainline unless a later Human Gate reverses that decision.

risks.summary: Downstream authors could treat target-expression or designed-but-unsurfaced UI language as authorization for new simulation/product systems.
risks.mitigation: Each derived task must cite existing read models, commands, or classify work as layout/copy/token/asset-slot only; any new protocol, schema, sim rule, or domain capability needs separate systems review and, where architectural, ADR/Human Gate routing.

risks.summary: Derived tasks can accidentally narrow the complete Human Gate list if they copy only the short visual handoff summary.
risks.mitigation: Lead must copy the full task JSON Human Gate list and systems review conditions into every downstream M7 UI/localization task.

risks.summary: Portraits, icons, frames, map symbols, title language, scripts, sacred imagery, clothing, architecture, weapons, flags, music, and ritual motifs can imply unsupported historical or cultural claims.
risks.mitigation: Use neutral placeholders, tokens, and replacement slots until historical/cultural review clears specific assets; major unresolved cases go to Human Gate.

risks.summary: The product directive and visual handoff name zh-CN, while existing M7 coverage records use zh-Hans for Simplified Chinese.
risks.mitigation: The downstream i18n foundation task must explicitly choose or alias locale identifiers through systems review and tests; no locale-code choice is approved by this handoff alone.

risks.summary: git status showed parallel M7 culture-review/project-state WIP before and after this review artifact was written, including modified state files and untracked culture-review handoff files.
risks.mitigation: Lead integration should stage only the intended visual/UX QA handoff for this route, reconcile or separately integrate culture-review state changes, and re-run status before acceptance.

requested_action: lead accept and integrate the visual/UX handoff, then create or update downstream M7 UI/localization quality-track tasks while preserving all conditions.
